item.name: CADI_Baseline_WebMap_WFL1
item.title: CADI_Baseline_WebMap_WFL1
item.snippet:
Clean Access Desert Initiative (CADI) baseline map for Wake and Durham Counties, North Carolina. 

This web map identifies spatial disparities in access to public restrooms by integrating restroom availability (density and count) with community vulnerability indicators, including poverty levels, vehicle access, and age-related vulnerability. 

Each census tract is classified into Low, Moderate, or High priority zones based on a composite CADI score (0–1), where higher values indicate greater need for improved restroom access. 

This map supports planning, equity analysis, and decision-making by highlighting areas with critical infrastructure gaps and enabling further scenario-based analysis through associated geoprocessing tools.

layerMetadata.name: CADI_Baseline_Priority_Zones
layerMetadata.description:
This layer presents the 2020 U.S. Census Tract boundaries of 
the United States in the 50 states and the District of Columbia. This layer is updated annually. The geography is sourced 
from U.S. Census Bureau 2020 TIGER FGDB (National Sub-State) and edited using TIGER Hydrography to add a detailed coastline for cartographic purposes. Attribute fields include 2020 total population from the U.S. Census Public Law 94 data.This
 ready-to-use layer can be used in ArcGIS Pro and in ArcGIS Online and 
its configurable apps, dashboards, StoryMaps, custom apps, and mobile 
apps. The data can also be exported for offline workflows. Cite the 
'U.S. Census Bureau' when using this data.
layerMetadata.copyrightText: Sources: Esri; U.S. Department of Commerce, Census Bureau; U.S. Department of Commerce (DOC), National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA), National Ocean Service (NOS), National Geodetic Survey (NGS)
